macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Major US Stock Indices Close Lower on March 19 Amid Rising Volatility
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.27% to 6606.49, Nasdaq 100 declined 0.29% to 24355.275, and Dow Jones dropped 0.44% to 65876.58. The VIX spiked to 25.09, indicating heightened market anxiety despite modest index declines and lighter-than-average trading volume.

items.title: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Raises 2026 Inflation Forecast
items.summary: The Fed maintained rates at 3.50-3.75% and raised its 2026 inflation forecast to 2.7%, with the dot plot projecting only one rate cut for the year. The decision triggered significant crypto liquidations and broader market selloffs.

items.title: Micron Technology Reports Record Q2 Fiscal Results, Sparks Sector Concerns
items.summary: Micron's fiscal Q2 2026 revenue nearly doubled year-over-year to $23.9 billion with adjusted EPS of $12.20, exceeding expectations. However, the company's aggressive spending plans raised concerns, contributing to pre-market declines in storage and semiconductor stocks.

items.title: Cryptocurrency Markets Decline Following Fed Decision
items.summary: Bitcoin fell approximately 5% to around $70,500 and XRP dropped to $1.45 following the Fed's rate decision. Over $158 million in leveraged long positions were liquidated within four hours of the announcement.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Price Drops to $69,370 on March 19 Amid Market Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin fell $3,113 to $69,370.14 by 9:45 a.m. ET on March 19, 2026, down from recent highs around $74,298, influenced by stalled U.S. crypto legislation and interest-rate expectations. Ether traded at about $2,121, reflecting ongoing price swings.

items.title: Mastercard to Acquire Stablecoin Firm BVNK for Up to $1.8 Billion
items.summary: Mastercard announced plans on March 17 to buy stablecoin infrastructure provider BVNK for up to $1.8 billion to expand blockchain-based payments, including cross-border remittances. This move highlights growing mainstream adoption of stablecoins.

items.title: SEC Issues Guidance Categorizing Tokens as Commodities, Stablecoins, and Securities
items.summary: The U.S. SEC released long-awaited guidance sorting crypto tokens into categories like digital commodities, stablecoins, and digital securities, impacting market dynamics. Citigroup lowered Bitcoin and Ether targets due to stalled U.S. crypto legislation.

items.title: Institutional Inflows into Crypto Hit $1.06 Billion for Third Straight Week
items.summary: CoinShares reported $1.06 billion in weekly inflows into digital-asset products on March 16, pushing assets under management to $140 billion despite geopolitical turmoil. Investors view Bitcoin increasingly as a macro hedge.

items.title: Standard Chartered Raises Ethereum Targets, Declares 2026 'Year of Ethereum'
items.summary: Standard Chartered named 2026 the year of Ethereum, boosting its year-end ETH target to $7,500 and 2028 projection to $25,000, citing aggressive developments. This comes amid Remittix presale approaching $30M raised.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Autoscience Raises $14M to Automate AI Research Labs
items.summary: Autoscience, a San Mateo-based AI startup, secured $14 million in seed funding led by General Catalyst and Toyota Ventures to develop AI systems that automate machine learning research, generating peer-reviewed papers and outperforming humans in Kaggle competitions. The funding addresses human capacity bottlenecks in AI research, targeting industries like finance and manufacturing.

items.title: Mistral Unveils Forge Platform for Custom AI Models
items.summary: Mistral launched Forge, a platform enabling enterprises and governments to build custom AI models trained from scratch on their data. Announced on March 18, 2026, it supports tailored AI development for specific organizational needs.

items.title: Tevogen.AI Leader to Speak on Agentic AI at FabCon 2026
items.summary: Tevogen CIO and Head of Tevogen.AI, Mittul Mehta, will present at Microsoft Fabric Community Conference on March 18-19, 2026, discussing agentic data engineering and modernizing data estates for enterprise AI. Sessions highlight adaptive pipelines and Tevogen's ML applications in biomedical discovery.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Ubisoft Closes Red Storm Entertainment, Lays Off 105 Employees
items.summary: Ubisoft has shut down Red Storm Entertainment, its longtime Cary-based studio, resulting in over 100 layoffs. The studio will cease game development operations.

items.title: Playstack Publisher Maintains 85% Hit Ratio with Strong 2025 Performance
items.summary: Playstack, publisher of the hit game Balatro, reported an 85% hit ratio in 2025 with over $100 million in lifetime Steam revenue. The publisher has eight titles scheduled for 2026 launch.

items.title: GAMIVO Expands In-Game Top-Ups Through Coda Partnership
items.summary: GAMIVO partnered with Coda to expand in-game top-up offerings, now providing access to over 1,000 in-game items for 100 popular titles with delivery times reduced from 30 minutes to minutes.

items.title: Video Game Stocks to Watch: Turtle Beach, Motorsport Games, Alliance Entertainment
items.summary: MarketBeat identified three video game stocks showing notable trading activity: Turtle Beach (gaming peripherals), Motorsport Games (racing titles), and Alliance Entertainment (entertainment distributor).

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
